name: IEEE ICICLE — Industry Consortium on Learning Engineering
focus: Professional standards body for learning engineering. Produces the LE Adoption Maturity Model, competency frameworks, annual conference proceedings, and the Journal of Learning Engineering. Chartered 2017 under IEEE LTSC.
notes: The institutional anchor of the modern LE field. Sponsors the annual ICICLE conference; co-sponsors the xAPI profile work; primary source for LE body of knowledge documentation.

name: The Simon Initiative — Carnegie Mellon University
focus: Cross-disciplinary learning engineering ecosystem at CMU. Encompasses LearnLab, OLI, DataShop/LearnSphere, and the METALS master's program. Mission: improve learning outcomes through rigorous research and technology deployment.
notes: Named for Herbert Simon. The world's most integrated LE research-to-practice ecosystem. Hundreds of faculty; world's largest educational log data bank; open-source tooling via OpenSimon.

name: Society for Learning Analytics Research
focus: Academic society promoting learning analytics research, practice, and policy. Organizes the annual LAK conference and Learning Analytics Summer Institute (LASI). Publishes the Journal of Learning Analytics.
notes: Founded 2011 alongside the first LAK conference. Emphasizes practice-oriented, ethical analytics as distinct from pure EDM.

name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics
focus: Research center at University of Pennsylvania directing EDM and LE policy work. Led by Ryan Baker. Produced 'High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering' (2021) and several landmark EDM papers.
notes: Key policy hub alongside academic research. Baker is editor of JEDM and a primary organizer of the EDM conference.

name: HCII — Human-Computer Interaction Institute, Carnegie Mellon
focus: Academic home of learning sciences and technologies at CMU. Houses Ken Koedinger, Al Corbett, and the PACT Center. Offers PhD and MS programs integrating HCI, learning sciences, and data science.
notes: One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. Co-originator of learning engineering as a named field.

name: International Educational Data Mining Society
focus: Society organizing the annual EDM conference and publishing the Journal of Educational Data Mining. Focus on computational, discovery-oriented analysis of educational data.
notes: EDM emphasizes mathematical and algorithmic approaches; complementary to but distinct from LA (practice-oriented).

name: Learning Sciences Research Institute, UIC
focus: Interdisciplinary research institute at University of Illinois Chicago spanning learning sciences, computational methods, and educational equity.

name: International Conference on Intelligent Tutoring Systems
focus: Premier venue for ITS research: cognitive models, student modeling, adaptive instruction, feedback algorithms. Founded 1988 in Montreal. Proceedings published by Springer LNCS.
notes: The oldest dedicated LE/ITS conference. ITS2026 in Cyprus (June 1–5, 2026).

name: International Conference on Learning Analytics and Knowledge
focus: Practice-oriented learning analytics, ethical deployment, dashboard design, predictive modeling, algorithmic fairness. Community-building for the SoLAR network.
notes: Emphasizes socio-technical context of analytics; complements EDM's algorithmic focus.

name: International Conference on Educational Data Mining
focus: Algorithmic and computational approaches to educational data: knowledge tracing, early warning systems, automated feature engineering, student behavior modeling.
notes: One of three major computational LE conference series (with LAK and AIED).

name: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Education
focus: AI-driven educational systems: generative AI tutors, NLP for learning, deep knowledge tracing, conversational agents, human-AI interaction in education.
notes: Largest first-authorship from China (vs. US dominance at LAK/EDM) — highlights globally distributed LE research ecosystem.

name: International Society of the Learning Sciences Annual Meeting
focus: Design-based research, socio-cultural learning theory, collaborative learning, epistemology of learning environments. Balances computational rigor with qualitative and humanistic perspectives.
notes: Provides the qualitative and socio-cultural counterbalance to EDM/LAK's algorithmic orientation. Important for ensuring LE systems remain human-centered.

name: Learning @ Scale
focus: Technical and pedagogical research on large-scale digital learning: MOOCs, online education platforms, at-scale experimentation, A/B testing in learning contexts.
notes: ACM-sponsored; focuses on the intersection of scale and learning quality. Home of 'doer effect' and A/B experimentation papers.

name: Journal of the Learning Sciences
focus: Multidisciplinary forum for theoretical models, epistemology, and design-based research in learning sciences. Publishes foundational work on socio-cognitive frameworks, knowledge-building, and interaction analysis.
notes: Home of Brown 1992 (design experiments, 2,800+ citations) and Scardamalia & Bereiter 1994 (knowledge-building, 1,800+ citations). The theoretical anchor journal for learning engineering.

name: Journal of Educational Data Mining
focus: Open-access journal dedicated to algorithms and methodologies for analyzing educational data: knowledge tracing, automated feature engineering, predictive student modeling.
notes: Premier venue for computational LE methods. Baker & Yacef 2009 (founding codification of EDM) published here. Diamond open access.

name: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
focus: Technical design and architecture of learning systems: adaptive algorithms, NLP conversational agents, multimodal telemetry (eye tracking, EEG, GSR), large-scale platform engineering.
notes: Requires substantive technical innovation in CS alongside pedagogical validity. Key venue for AI-in-education engineering papers.

name: Journal of Learning Analytics
focus: Socio-technical implications of educational data: dashboard design, algorithmic ethics, data privacy, human-in-the-loop architectures, equity in analytics deployment.
notes: Official publication of SoLAR. Emphasizes practice implications and ethical framing alongside technical analysis.

name: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
focus: Premier venue for ITS, generative AI in education, deep cognitive modeling, evaluation of AI vs. human tutoring. Algorithmic accuracy evaluations and human factors studies.
notes: Springer. The home journal for rigorous AIED research between conference cycles.

name: Journal of Learning Engineering
focus: Applied integration of learning design, analytics, and systems thinking. Provides the 'Learning Engineering Primer' for terminological alignment. Diamond Open Access; community-driven by ICICLE.
notes: The only journal specifically dedicated to learning engineering as a named field. Diamond OA means no publication fees for authors or readers.

name: International Journal of STEM Education
focus: Discipline-based education research (DBER): problem-based learning, flipped classrooms, educational robotics, STEM learning outcomes at scale.
notes: Springer Open. Critical venue for LE researchers designing domain-specific systems in STEM contexts.

name: IEEE 1484.1-2003: Learning Technology Systems Architecture (LTSA)
focus: Defines the high-level system components and communication protocols required for interoperable learning technology platforms. Foundational systems architecture standard for the field.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC.

name: IEEE 9274 Family (xAPI / Experience API)
focus: Data instrumentation standard governing how learning experiences are tracked, stored in Learning Record Stores (LRS), and analyzed across distributed platforms. Includes JSON-LD application profiles, cybersecurity protocols, and vocabulary registries.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC / ADL. The data infrastructure standard underpinning modern LE telemetry pipelines.

name: IEEE P2247.1 & P2247.2: Adaptive Instructional Systems (AIS)
focus: Classifies components, parameters, and interoperability protocols for adaptive instructional systems. Engineering blueprints for building systems that dynamically adjust instruction based on real-time learner state.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC. The most directly LE-relevant IEEE standard family.

name: IEEE 1484.20.2: Recommended Practice for Defining Competencies
focus: Rigorous data schema for defining human competencies. Supports Integrated Learner Records (ILR) spanning K-12, higher education, workforce, and military environments.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC. Critical for competency-based education and credential portability.

name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
focus: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

name: Open Context Exchange (OCX)
focus: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing.
notes: Developed by Learning Tapestry / ICICLE. Enables interoperable OER ecosystems.
